title: Preparing mitochondrial samples for immunoblot analysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dbgae","text":"Protocol for preparation of mitochondrial samples for immunoblot analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Thaw mitochondrial stocks on ice, and aliquot out the desired amount of mitochondria.
Centrifuge each aliquot for 00:10:00 10000 x g, 4 °C,
Carefully aspirate the supernatant from each sample.

Add a volume of 1x SDS sample buffer (5% w/v SDS, 10% v/v glycerol, 100 mM DTT, 50 mM Tris-Cl pH 6.8) equal to the amount of mitochondria (in ug) to each sample. Eg. If each sample contains 20 µg of mitochondria, add 20 µL of 1x SDS sample buffer.
Vortex samples for ~5 seconds to mix, and then boil at 99 °C with shaking at max speed for 00:10:00 .
Allow samples to cool to room temperature, quickly centrifuge to collect liquid to the bottom of the tube, and vortex for ~3 seconds to ensure the samples are homogenous.
Samples can now be directly loaded onto an SDS-PAGE gel, or stored at -20 °C for later use.
creator: Louise Uoselis
